buy_side_bullets: Capital wars and Big Cycle Stage 5 underway — diversify away from dollar bonds into gold/commodities. [Dalio]
US economy 'strong and getting stronger' — stay long US equities; hedge dollar via copper. [Druckenmiller]
AI bull market has 'another year or two' — 1999 analog; recently added to AI stocks. [Tudor Jones]
High-conviction rotation into cyclicals and China exposure; memory chips drove 32% H1 2026 gain. [Tepper]
No Fed rate cuts possible; DoubleLine at lowest risk in 17 years — hike is more likely than cut. [Gundlach]
sell_side_bullets: S&P above 6,300 in July triggers sell signal; $56.4B into money markets vs only $2.2B equities week of July 2 — risk-off bifurcation. [Hartnett]
Surging bank lending + M2 growth risks rekindling inflation — double-digit inflation most probable 3-5 year outcome. [Edwards evolving]
2027 recession call: AI spending peak in 2026 tips economy; labor market already deteriorating below pre-pandemic hiring rates. [Rosenberg]
Goldman cuts 12-month US recession probability to 15% from 25%; H2 2026 GDP nudged up to 2% on AI wealth effect, lower gas prices. [Hatzius]
S&P 500 target 8,250 — 'FEMA: fabulous earnings momentum'; Roaring 2020s base case raised to 60%. [Yardeni]
whats_new: Goldman cuts 12-month US recession probability to 15% from 25% [Hatzius shift]
Hartnett: S&P 6,300 is the July sell trigger; week July 2 flows show risk-off bifurcation [Hartnett evolving]
Rosenberg shifts recession call from 2026 to 2027 via AI spending peak thesis [Rosenberg shift]
Dalio fully exits Bridgewater in July 2026 after 52-year career [Dalio shift]
Einhorn closes Greenlight to new investors; adopts macro instruments over equities [Einhorn shift]
vs_market_buy: Dalio and Einhorn more bearish on US equities than market consensus pricing
Gundlach pricing in Fed hike while market still pricing eventual cuts
Bass sees China-Taiwan risk as more imminent than market is pricing
vs_market_sell: Rosenberg sees 2027 recession risk that market is broadly ignoring
Edwards sees double-digit inflation as probable vs market's soft-landing pricing
Bannister's 3.3% EPS growth far below the double-digit consensus for 2026
buy_vs_sell_divergence: Rates: Gundlach sees Fed hike as more likely than cut; Hatzius/Goldman cut recession prob and favor H2 recovery — fundamentally different Fed paths
AI valuation: Tudor Jones/Ackman/Yardeni constructive; Einhorn/Hartnett/Berezin see bubble conditions and extreme valuations
Inflation: Edwards (double-digit coming), Rosenberg (disinflation to below target), Slok (stuck near 3%) — three incompatible scenarios
Timeline: Rosenberg 2027 recession via AI peak; Berezin 30% probability next 12 months; Hatzius only 15%
aligned: AI capex as dominant 2026 macro driver — buy and sell side both acknowledge but disagree on sustainability
Dollar hedging is warranted — multiple investors across both sides diversifying away from USD concentration
Gold as defensive hedge — Einhorn, Rosenberg, Bannister, Dalio all holding gold as a core position
Labor market conditions are critical leading indicator — both sides watching for deterioration signals
buy_side_trades: Long US equities; long copper as dollar hedge [Druckenmiller]
Long memory chips (Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix); rotating to cyclicals and China [Tepper]
Diversify into gold, commodities, non-US equities away from dollar bonds [Dalio]
Long SaaS (Adobe, Autodesk, Veeva); long MSFT Dec 2028 LEAPs; short NVDA/PLTR via puts [Burry]
Long gold and copper directly; rates positioning via macro instruments [Einhorn]
sell_side_trades: Sell S&P 500 above 6,300; long money markets and bonds over equities [Hartnett]
Long gold and silver; long bonds for disinflation + recession scenario [Rosenberg]
Overweight healthcare, staples, gold vs Big Tech hedge [Bannister]
Long Bitcoin on 200-day MA recapture >$70K trigger [Visser]
Long bonds as AI bust hedge; underweight equities on asymmetric downside [Berezin]
dominant_themes: AI capex cycle sustainability and bubble risk
Capital wars, dollar diversification, and geopolitical risk
